tools.name: File Manager
tools.description: Filesystem MCP in a container. Add folders in MCP Tools; each mounts at /app/<folder-name>. Install works before any folder is set.

commands.name: read_text_file
commands.description: Read a file as UTF-8 text; optional head/tail line limits

commands.name: read_media_file
commands.description: Read image or audio as base64 with MIME type

commands.name: read_multiple_files
commands.description: Read several files in one call

commands.name: edit_file
commands.description: Pattern-based selective edits with optional dry run

commands.name: list_directory_with_sizes
commands.description: List directory with sizes and optional sort

commands.name: move_file
commands.description: Move or rename a file or directory

commands.name: search_files
commands.description: Recursive glob search under a path

commands.name: directory_tree
commands.description: Recursive JSON tree; requires absolute `path` under a mounted root (e.g. /app/<folder>)

tools.name: Fetch
tools.description: Official MCP fetch server: fetches URLs and returns markdown-friendly content. By default the server respects robots.txt. To opt in for this install, set catalog field ignore_robots_txt to true (appends --ignore-robots-txt) or add that flag under mcp_server_cmd in advanced config — only if you accept bypassing robots for all fetched hosts. The robots_ignore_allowlist field is reserved for future per-host policy and is currently informational.

commands.name: fetch
commands.description: Fetch a URL and return extracted content (markdown by default)

tools.name: Git
tools.description: Official MCP git server: status, diff, log, branches, and commits against repos under your mounted folders (use paths like /app/<folder>).

commands.name: git_status
commands.description: Working tree status for a repository

commands.name: git_diff
commands.description: Diff against a branch or commit

commands.name: git_log
commands.description: Commit history with optional date filters

tools.name: Sequential Thinking
tools.description: Official MCP sequential-thinking server for structured step-by-step reasoning.

commands.name: sequentialthinking
commands.description: Record and refine a sequence of reasoning steps

tools.name: Time
tools.description: Official MCP time server: current time and timezone conversions.

tools.name: Brave Search
tools.description: Brave Search MCP: web, local, video, image, news, and summarizer tools via the Brave Search API. After install, add BRAVE_API_KEY under Tool Engine → Container secrets (saved in mcp.json). Alternatively, export BRAVE_API_KEY in the shell before you start Pengine. Requires outbound network.

commands.name: brave_web_search
commands.description: Web search with filtering, pagination, and optional AI summarization

commands.name: brave_local_search
commands.description: Local business and place discovery with ratings, hours, and AI descriptions

commands.name: brave_video_search
commands.description: Video search with metadata and thumbnails

commands.name: brave_news_search
commands.description: News search with freshness controls and breaking-news indicators

commands.name: brave_summarizer
commands.description: AI-powered summaries from web search results

tools.name: Shell
tools.description: Sandboxed shell-exec MCP. Runs commands inside the container; only paths you bind via MCP folders are visible to it. Pair with `pengine/file-manager` so the model can build, test, and run code it just wrote (Claude Code `Bash` parity).

commands.name: shell_execute
commands.description: Run a shell command (foreground / background / detached / adaptive)

commands.name: process_get_execution
commands.description: Detailed execution info for a previous run

commands.name: shell_set_default_workdir
commands.description: Set the default working directory for subsequent shell calls

commands.name: process_terminate
commands.description: Terminate a process with a specific signal

commands.name: process_monitor
commands.description: Start real-time monitoring for a running process

commands.name: terminal_create
commands.description: Create an interactive PTY-backed terminal session

commands.name: terminal_send_input
commands.description: Send input (with control codes) to a terminal

commands.name: get_cleanup_suggestions
commands.description: Recommend output files to clean up

commands.name: perform_auto_cleanup
commands.description: Apply retention policies to captured outputs

commands.name: monitoring_get_stats
commands.description: System-wide stats from the shell MCP

tools.name: Ripgrep
tools.description: High-throughput content search across mounted folders via the `rg` binary (Claude Code `Grep` parity). Use with `pengine/file-manager` for navigation; this server is read-only.

commands.name: search
commands.description: ripgrep search with regex, file-type filters, and context lines

commands.name: advanced-search
commands.description: Search with extra ripgrep flags (multiline, fixed-strings, glob, …)

commands.name: list-files
commands.description: List files ripgrep would search under a path (respects .gitignore)

commands.name: list-file-types
commands.description: List the file-type aliases ripgrep understands

tools.name: Language Server (LSP)
tools.description: Code intelligence via cclsp: go-to-definition, find-references, rename, diagnostics. Image bakes in TypeScript, Python, Go, and Rust LSPs (typescript-language-server, pylsp, gopls, rust-analyzer). Extend by deriving from this image and editing `/mcp/cclsp.json`.

commands.name: find_definition
commands.description: Find the definition of a symbol by name and kind in a file

commands.name: find_references
commands.description: Find all references to a symbol across the workspace

commands.name: rename_symbol
commands.description: Rename a symbol across the workspace (supports dry-run)

commands.name: rename_symbol_strict
commands.description: Rename a symbol at a specific position in a file

commands.name: get_diagnostics
commands.description: Errors / warnings / hints for a file from the language server

commands.name: restart_server
commands.description: Restart one or all backing LSP servers

tools.name: Notebook
tools.description: Jupyter-notebook (`.ipynb`) editor MCP — create, edit, move, search cells without a running kernel (Claude Code `NotebookEdit` parity). Operates on files under your mounted folders; `--allow-root /app` is baked in.

commands.name: notebook_create
commands.description: Create a new empty notebook file

commands.name: notebook_add_cell
commands.description: Insert a code/markdown cell after a given index

commands.name: notebook_delete_cell
commands.description: Delete a cell at a given index

tools.name: Memory
tools.description: Official MCP memory server: knowledge-graph style entities, relations, and observations; state file is stored in a dedicated folder (default under app data, or pick your own).

commands.name: create_entities
commands.description: Create entities in the knowledge graph
